title: God Forgives The Streets Don't 2
description: God Forgives The Streets Don't 2 is a book by Blake Karrington. The exported reason says: 2 ‐ Facilitates unauthorized organizational activity;#10 ‐ Sexually Explicit Materials;#7 ‐ Contains Security Threat Group material;#3 ‐ Encourages/instructs commission of criminal activity
descriptionParagraphs: God Forgives The Streets Don't 2 is a book by Blake Karrington. The exported reason says: 2 ‐ Facilitates unauthorized organizational activity;#10 ‐ Sexually Explicit Materials;#7 ‐ Contains Security Threat Group material;#3 ‐ Encourages/instructs commission of criminal activity

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Kansas. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The Kansas prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: 2 ‐ Facilitates. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: God Forgives The Streets Don't 2 is a book by Blake Karrington. The exported reason says: 2 ‐ Facilitates unauthorized organizational activity;#10 ‐ Sexually Explicit Materials;#7 ‐ Contains Security Threat Group material;#3 ‐ Encourages/instructs commission of criminal activity
banningParagraphs: God Forgives The Streets Don't 2 entered censorship debates as a book associated with morality, print scandal, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around instructional harm, obscenity, public morality, and public order.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2022-01-13 in Kansas, where Kansas corrections agencies and prison mailrooms excluded from prison circulation. The Kansas prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime. The exported reason says: 2 ‐ Facilitates. The Kansas prison-ban record treats the book as excluded reading inside state custody, which shows how prison and mailroom censorship function as a government reading regime.
